findings.title: Wrapping off+n producer forges an unbounded in-bounds window (bounds elision -> OOB)
findings.minimal_fix: Pin the sole legal (b) producer to a non-wrapping sum and make the engine bind the window's upper endpoint to THAT binding: accept only `check ile<u64>(iadd.trap<u64>(off,n), len(s))` (overflow traps before the check) or the Ok-arm binding of `iadd.checked<u64>(off,n)`; REJECT any window whose off+n term is produced by `iadd.wrap` or by any value not proven overflow-free. Delete the ill-typed `iadd.checked-proven` exemplar. Do not adopt until the non-overflow discharge is a defined, checkable rule.

findings.title: Runtime bounds trap `off + K/8 > len(s)` wraps at the u64 edge -> OOB even with elision OFF
findings.detail: LOAD-1 specifies the out-of-range trap as `off + K/8 > len(s)` with off:u64 and K/8 in {1,2,4,8}. Computed in u64 this add wraps: at off = u64::MAX, off + 2 = 1, and `1 > len(s)` is false for any len(s) >= 1, so the trap does NOT fire and load_le_u16 reads 2 bytes at base+2^64-1 (wild OOB read). This is the RUNTIME backstop, so it is unsound independently of DOM-1: when the fact channel fails closed (elision OFF), this retained check is the SOLE guard and it is wrong -- fail-closed does not save it. The offset is realistically attacker-influenced (a TLV/validated-view parser reads an 8-byte length or offset field from the buffer, then loads at it). Note the existing `index<T>` bounds check compares `i < len` with no addition and never had this hole; LOAD-1 introduces the add. It compounds with the DOM-1(b) finding: F1 removes the checker-level guard, this removes the runtime guard -- together a guaranteed OOB.
findings.minimal_fix: Never spell the guard as `off + K/8`. Specify: trap iff `len(s) < K/8` OR `off > len(s) - K/8`, computing `len(s) - K/8` only under the `len(s) >= K/8` guard (no underflow). Equivalently the pass condition is `len(s) >= K/8 AND off <= len(s) - K/8`. Pin this exact form in the codegen corpus.

findings.title: Length-domination facts survive a helper call that resizes the place by &uniq (cross-call UAF/OOB)
findings.minimal_fix: Either (preferred, minimal) restrict DOM-1 windows to non-resizable backing places only -- buffer<T>, slice<'r,T>, array<T,N> -- which covers C2 and C8 and removes every resize op from the picture; OR add an explicit invalidator: any call receiving the fact's place by &uniq (or any mode admitting a length write) kills its length-domination facts unless the callee signature proves the length unchanged. State it normatively, not as an assumption.

findings.title: 'K and V must be clone' cites a kernel class that does not exist; owning-V cost model has no clone operation to lower to
findings.detail: The row rejects 'an affine V [that] has no clone,' but the kernel defines no clone capability: OWN-1 defines only copy vs affine; there is no Clone contract and no `*_clone` op for owned composites (the only `_clone` rows are cq_tx_clone/cq_rx_clone endpoint peers, CQ-3, unrelated). So 'must be clone' cites a class the checker cannot test -- it is an assumption, not an enforceable rule -- and there is no per-element clone operation to lower an owning-V clone to. Yet the draft's own cost model ('for owning V it is n element clones + n allocations, ~30-3000 ns/entry') and open-question (a) presuppose owning-V cloning, and C5's owning-value COW story depends on it. The row is internally inconsistent: either V is restricted to copy (enforceable via OWN-1, but then the owning-V cost model and the KB-value COW regime are dead), or it needs a Clone contract this delta does not introduce. Note the loan-coexistence angle is separately SOUND (see failed-attack note).
findings.minimal_fix: For this delta restrict tbl_clone to copy K and copy V, citing OWN-1 (identical to seq_extend_copy's 'T copy required'); delete the owning-V cost/allocation model. Split owning-V clone into a separate delta that FIRST introduces a checkable Clone contract (a capability predicate plus a per-type clone op) and passes its own review.

findings.title: 'one unaligned machine load' not pinned to align-1; a natural-alignment lowering is UB
findings.detail: The CG contract says 'exactly one unaligned machine load' but does not pin a concrete alignment. If lowered as a natural-alignment typed load (LLVM `load i16, ptr %p` with default align 2) at a byte offset that is not 2-aligned, that is UB: LLVM may assume the pointer aligned and miscompile, and strict-alignment targets fault. The 'codegen-identical to the C idiom' claim is only true for the memcpy/packed idiom (byte alignment), NOT a `uint16_t*` deref (itself C UB for unaligned). The draft is correct that no alignment FACT is needed -- but only because the lowering must be align-1; that is unstated. The corpus asm-diff is pinned on the deployment target only (open question (b) already flags split-cache-line), not on a strict-alignment target.
findings.minimal_fix: Pin the CG contract to LLVM `load iK, ptr, align 1` (or the memcpy-to-temp equivalent); state normatively that LOAD-1 needs NO alignment fact BECAUSE it lowers at align 1; add a strict-alignment-target (e.g. an ARM config) asm-diff pin, not only x86.

findings.title: Canonical producer `check ile(iadd.checked-proven off+n, len(s))` does not parse or typecheck
findings.detail: Surface symptom of the FATAL F1: `iadd.checked-proven` is not an OP-1 operation (no such mode suffix; the closed set is wrap/trap/checked/sat/strict per FORM-3), and the real `iadd.checked` returns Result<T,Overflow>, which cannot be the operand of `ile<u64>`. The exemplar as written is un-implementable and steers an implementer toward the exploitable `iadd.wrap` spelling.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace the exemplar with the pinned non-wrapping producer from the F1 fix (iadd.trap sum, or the Ok-arm of iadd.checked).

findings.title: len=0 mask-nothing forge is BLOCKED (pow2 producer excludes 0)
findings.detail: Attempted the len=0 edge: x & (len-1) = x & (0-1) = x, masking nothing, to get an unbounded masked index. BLOCKED. The pow2 producer is `ieq(ipopcount<u64>(len), 1_u32)`; popcount(0)=0 != 1, so len=0 can never pass, and OP-5 checks are never elided, so a runtime len=0 traps at the check. The masked-nothing index is unreachable through the fact. Survived.
findings.minimal_fix: None required. Optional hardening: state explicitly in DOM-1(a) that pow2(len) excludes len=0 (popcount==1 already gives this) so the property is self-documenting.

findings.title: Returning `own uK` smuggles no borrow/provenance past region 'r
findings.detail: Attempted to smuggle a borrow or aliasing provenance out of the slice's region 'r via the load result. BLOCKED. load_le/be return `own uK`, a primitive copy value with no region parameter and no borrow; the effect row `reads('r)` bounds the read to the call; the result is disjoint from 'r and carries nothing that outlives it. No lifetime channel exists here. Survived. (The live soundness hazards for LOAD-1 are the off+K/8 overflow and the alignment pin, above -- not the return type.)

findings.title: Clone under a live loan on t is sound (R6 blocks the dangerous case)
findings.detail: Attempted to clone t while a loan on t is live. BLOCKED/sound: a live `tbl_entry` token holds a uniq loan and freezes t (R6: while a uniq entry is live, no borrow mint from the place is permitted), so the `&'r` mint tbl_clone requires is rejected while the token lives -- they cannot coexist. A live shared value loan (from tbl_get, &'r) CAN coexist, but tbl_clone only reads t and returns a fresh independent table, so two concurrent reads are fine and the source is untouched. The 'loan NONE + &'r receiver' reasoning holds. Survived. (The real tbl_clone defect is the clone-vs-copy underspecification, above.)
findings.minimal_fix: None required for the loan interaction.

lens: SOUNDNESS -- fact-channel forgery (80% on DOM-1 bounds-elision forging and LOAD-1 overflow/alignment edges)

verdict_per_delta.delta2_tbl_clone: REVISE -- one MAJOR. No fact channel and the loan/freeze reasoning is sound (R6 blocks clone-under-uniq-loan; shared-loan coexistence is read-only-safe). But 'K and V must be clone' cites a nonexistent kernel class: v0 has copy (OWN-1), no Clone contract and no per-element clone op. Restrict to copy V for this delta and drop the owning-V cost model, or split owning-V clone into a delta that first adds a Clone capability. Not adoptable verbatim.
verdict_per_delta.delta3_LOAD1: DO NOT ADOPT -- FATAL. The runtime bounds trap `off + K/8 > len(s)` wraps at off=u64::MAX and admits an OOB read even with elision OFF (fail-closed does not save it); reachable with a data-derived offset in the C8 validated-view use case. Plus its elision path inherits the DOM-1(b) FATAL, and the 'unaligned load' lowering is not pinned to align-1 (UB risk). Fix the trap arithmetic (subtraction form), pin align-1, and gate on DOM-1's fix.
verdict_per_delta.delta4_DOM1: DO NOT ADOPT -- FATAL. This is the bounds-eliding fact channel and it forges. (b): the off+n non-overflow obligation is named but not dischargeable -- the canonical producer is ill-typed and the nearest legal producer `iadd.wrap` forges an unbounded in-bounds window (concrete program supplied). Cross-call resize invalidation is unspecified: a helper that resizes the place by &uniq does not kill the caller's window/pow2 fact under the closed invalidator list + SEQ-6 (FATAL-class UAF supplied). The len=0 pow2 edge is correctly blocked. Pin a non-wrapping producer with engine endpoint-binding, and either restrict windows to non-resizable backing or write an explicit cross-call kill rule, before any adoption.

lens: FIDELITY AND COMPLETENESS — do the drafted rules actually enforce what their rationale claims, and does each invalidator/producer/kill set cover every path in optables.md S.1/S.2 and every interaction with R1-R15/AMD-1..5, OP-4/5, FN-8, CAT-2? Scope: deltas 2 (tbl_clone), 3 (LOAD-1), 4 (DOM-1). Delta 1 (concurrency) and the mid-review-appended Delta 5 (BRAND-1) are out of scope.

findings.title: Invalidator set omits opaque user-fn calls that resize through a passed &uniq borrow — no cross-call fact havoc exists anywhere in the kernel
findings.detail: DOM-1's invalidators are enumerated as PRIMITIVE length-writing ops ('grow, reserve, clear, truncate, a resizing store'). I grepped the entire M2 mass, RULES-RATIFIED, and kernel-spec-v0.6: NO rule havocs facts across a call. The kill discipline is 100% per-op-row `kills` columns (CAT-2, SEQ-6); a user-`fn` call's effect row carries zero fact-kill semantics, and CQ-6 only disclaims fact-crossing for concurrency ops (implying ordinary facts DO survive ordinary calls). Facts are per-binding and path-local. So a DOM-1 fact on a place passed by &uniq into a helper that resizes it internally survives the call. Concrete: `fn shrink['e](s: &uniq 'e seq<u64,0>) -> own unit writes('e) { let d: own Option<u64> = seq_pop(s); return unit; }` and `fn read['e](s: &uniq 'e seq<u64,0>, off: own u64) { let n = seq_len(&'e s); /*=4*/ let lim = iadd.checked<u64>(off, 2_u64); match lim { Ok(value: hi) => { check ile<u64>(hi, n) else trap; /* DOM-1(b): in_bounds(s,2),in_bounds(s,3) with off=2 */ shrink(s: &uniq 'e s); /* len 4->3, INB killed only inside shrink's env, not caller's */ let v: &u64 = seq_get(s, 3_u64); /* fact in_bounds(s,3) still live in caller -> OP-4 check elided -> OOB read */ ... } Err(...) => {...} } }`. seq_pop's `kills` (INB(s,*)) fires in shrink's fact environment; the caller's INB(s,3) is untouched by any stated rule. This defeats OP-4 and thus D1. The draft's own hostile flag lists 'a resize hidden behind a conformer call' as a class to survive — this review confirms the rule text does NOT close it.
findings.minimal_fix: Add a normative call-interaction clause to DOM-1: any call whose effect row includes writes(R) where R reaches the fact's place (or any overlapping place) KILLS all DOM-1 fact families on that place, unless the callee signature carries an explicit length-preservation obligation (none exists in v0, so: unconditional havoc on any writes-reaching call). State it as a rule, not an appositive; the ring/C8 cards remain sound because their facts live within a single op body that calls no resizer.

findings.title: Base out-of-range trap predicate `off + K/8 > len(s)` is computed in u64 and wraps — the RUNTIME safety check itself fails to trap on a near-max offset (independent of any elision)
findings.detail: LOAD-1 (line 205): 'an out-of-range access (`off + K/8 > len(s)`) traps'; discharge (line 206) 'proves `off + K/8 <= len(s)`'. Both are u64 arithmetic on a u64 `off`. For zero-copy parsing, `off` is derived from untrusted input bytes, so it is attacker-influenced. Concrete: `load_le_u32(s, off)` with off = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE (2^64-2), K/8 = 4. `off + 4` wraps to 2. `2 > len(s)` is false for any len>=2, so NO trap fires, and the load reads 4 bytes at byte offset 2^64-2 -> wild OOB read. This is the BASE op semantics being wrong, not a fact-channel elision: even with facts fully disabled, LOAD-1 as specified miscompiles the guard. The same wrap then also poisons the discharge condition and DOM-1(b)'s window (see the overflow finding).
findings.minimal_fix: Rewrite both LOAD-1 predicates to a non-wrapping form: trap-unless `len(s) >= K/8 AND off <= len(s) - K/8` (the subtraction is safe under the first conjunct), or evaluate `off + K/8` in the checker's unbounded/symbolic domain. Never a u64 `off + K/8` comparison. K/8 is a monomorphization constant, so `len - K/8` is one subtraction.

findings.title: Producer accepts more than the rationale intends: it cites a non-existent op, does not mechanically reject a wrapping-add check, and cannot be spelled non-wrapping inside a `requires` block
findings.detail: Three fidelity defects in the (b) producer. (1) The producer form is written `check ile(iadd.checked-proven off+n, len(s))` (line 255) — `iadd.checked-proven` is not a kernel op; `iadd.checked` returns `own Result<u64,Overflow>`, not a u64 comparable by `ile`. The rule prescribes syntax that does not parse. (2) The non-overflow requirement is a side-clause ('the `off + n` arithmetic must be proven non-overflowing', line 270), NOT part of the producer's admission test. Nothing stated rejects `check ile(iadd.wrap<u64>(off, n), len)` from producing the fact: with off = 2^64-1, n = 2, `iadd.wrap` = 1, `ile(1, len)` passes, and in_bounds(s,i) for off<=i<off+n is forged — then `index(s, off)` at off = 2^64-1 is a claimed-in-bounds OOB. (3) The rule names a `requires` prologue [FN-8] as a legal (b) producer, but FN-8 admits only non-trapping/total/pure ops; the only u64-returning adds are `iadd.wrap` (wraps -> unsound) and `iadd.sat` (sound, saturates high so the `ile` fails on overflow — but unmentioned); `iadd.checked` is unusable (its Result can't be matched in a requires block, control flow is forbidden) and `iadd.trap` is forbidden (trapping). So the prescribed `.checked` spelling is impossible in the very producer the rule blesses, and the naive `.wrap` spelling is forgeable.
findings.minimal_fix: Define the (b) producer precisely: the fact is produced ONLY by a `check ile(SUM, len<T>(s))` (or ige) where SUM is the Ok-arm payload of `iadd.checked<u64>(off,n)`, OR `iadd.sat<u64>(off,n)`, OR a compile-const-proven-non-wrapping sum — and explicitly deny production from `iadd.wrap`. For the requires-block path, pin the spelling to `iadd.sat` (the only non-trapping total add that makes overflow fail the check). Delete `iadd.checked-proven`.

findings.title: Conclusion has a free variable `b`; the mask-length-equals-current-buffer-length tie is unstated; and the rationale misattributes the bound to pow2 when the safety-sufficient premise is len != 0
findings.detail: DOM-1(a) (line 253): premise mentions only `len`; conclusion `in_bounds(b, iand(x, isub.wrap(len,1)))` introduces `b` unbound — the rule as written cannot be applied because it never says `len = len(b)`. The real soundness core is (i) len(b) != 0 and (ii) the mask is `len(b)-1` for b's CURRENT tracked length. Note x & (len-1) <= len-1 < len holds for ANY len>=1, pow2 or not — so pow2 is SUFFICIENT (pow2 => len>=1) but is NOT the safety-load-bearing invariant; pow2 is a correctness (ring-modulo) requirement. The rationale 'a masked index x & (len-1) is always < len' attributes the bound to the pow2 producer, which misdirects an implementer away from the actual obligation: that the `len` inside the mask is provably the SAME tracked quantity as the buffer's length at the index site. If the mask is computed from a different length binding (aliased length) or a stale one, x & (stale_len-1) can exceed current len -> OOB, while pow2 still 'holds'. This is the 'aliased length binding' attack the hostile flag names, and the rule text does not bind it.
findings.minimal_fix: State the producer as: a passed `check ieq(ipopcount<u64>(len<T>(b)), 1_u32)` for a place `b`, producing pow2(len(b)); and admit the derived in_bounds(b, iand(x, isub.wrap(L,1))) ONLY where L is provably len(b) at the index site (a live LEN(b)=L fact tied to the same b). Add a sentence that the safety-sufficient premise is len(b)!=0 and that pow2 is the correctness superset; the masked-length must equal the current tracked len(b).

findings.title: The new fact families are killed only in DOM-1 prose, never wired into the S.1/S.2 `kills` columns the checker consults, and the invalidator appositive contradicts its own CAT-2 citation
findings.detail: DOM-1 introduces two new fact families (POW2-mask producer and the LEN-window/off+n<=len producer) and says length writes 'KILL both fact families [CAT-2 kill discipline]'. But enforcement in this codebase is per-row `kills` columns, and NO S.1/S.2 row lists POW2 or LEN-window in its kills column (those families postdate optables). Existing rows kill INB(s,*) (so the DERIVED in_bounds dies on a primitive decrease — good) but leave the PRODUCER facts alive: e.g. `seq_truncate` kills 'INB(s,*); ELEM(s)' but not a pow2/mask-tie fact, so after `seq_truncate` to a smaller length a later index site can RE-DERIVE in_bounds from the surviving stale producer -> OOB even intra-procedurally. Separately, the appositive list ('grow, reserve, clear, truncate, a resizing store') is non-exhaustive versus optables (misses pop, remove_at, drain, take_all as decreasers) and its '[CAT-2 kill discipline]' citation conflicts with CAT-2's 'INB facts survive length-increasing ops' — the governing predicate 'any op that writes the place's length' is correct, but a checker keyed to the list-plus-CAT-2 gets contradictory guidance.
findings.minimal_fix: Amend every length-writing S.1/S.2 row's `kills` column to add the POW2 and LEN-window producer families (or state normatively that these producers are re-evaluated against current len rather than monotone-cached). Replace the appositive list with 'every row whose facts/kills column touches LEN(x)'. Reconcile with CAT-2 explicitly: the derived INB may survive a length increase; the pow2/LEN-window PRODUCERS die on any length write.

findings.title: CG contract 'one unaligned machine load' is not pinned to align-1 semantics; on strict-alignment targets this is UB / a hardware fault, and 'codegen-identical to the C idiom' is ambiguous
findings.detail: LOAD-1 (line 210) lowers a discharged load to 'exactly one unaligned machine load ... codegen-identical to the C idiom'. `s: slice<'r,u8>` has only byte alignment. A sound lowering must emit an explicitly-unaligned load (LLVM `load iK, ptr %p, align 1`), which LLVM legalizes per-target (single mov on x86; a safe sequence on strict-alignment targets). If the compiler emits a naturally-aligned load (default align K/8) from the byte pointer, that is LLVM-level UB and faults on strict-alignment or SIMD paths. 'The C idiom' is itself ambiguous: `memcpy(&tmp,p,K/8)` is the sound portable idiom, `*(uint32_t*)p` is UB. Open question (b) frames this as a performance question ('penalty-free unaligned ... split-cache-line note'), missing the correctness/UB axis entirely. This does NOT need a per-value alignment fact — the fix is purely in lowering.
findings.minimal_fix: Pin the CG contract to an LLVM `align 1` load (the memcpy-equivalent unaligned idiom), per-target legalized; add the pin to the codegen corpus asm-diff. State that strict-alignment targets lower to the safe sequence, not a trap and not UB. Re-file open-Q (b) as a correctness note, not performance.

findings.title: 'clone' names a predicate that does not exist in the kernel; the affine-V rejection contradicts the owning-V cost model, and an owning-V clone has no mechanism (shallow fallback aliases owned storage -> double free)
findings.detail: The row requires 'K and V must be clone (compile reject otherwise ... an affine V has no clone and is rejected)'. I confirmed there is NO Clone contract in the prelude/spec/optables — the only classification is OWN-1 copy vs affine. So 'clone' is unevaluable; only 'copy' is. The row then contradicts itself: it rejects affine V, yet the Cost section (lines 176-178) prices 'owning V ... n element clones + n allocations (~30-3000 ns/entry)' and CG-CLONE has 'a serial per-entry clone loop for owning V' — but every owning composite V (box, seq, nested table) IS affine, i.e. exactly what the row says it rejects. There is no `box_clone`/`seq_clone`/`tbl_clone`-of-V mechanism in optables, so owning-V deep clone is unspecified. If an implementer fills the gap with a shallow bitwise copy, the fresh table and the original SHARE the owned V storage -> double free at drop, violating D1's no-double-free. The bytes-key case is also unhandled: bytes K = buffer<u8> is affine and must be byte-copied for a sound clone, but 'K must be clone' glosses it.
findings.minimal_fix: For this slice, restrict tbl_clone to OWN-1 `copy` K and V (clone == the ~1-2 ns/entry control-byte+slot bulk memcpy); state the predicate as 'copy' not 'clone', which makes the affine-V/affine-K rejection a real enforceable rule. Delete or mark-DEFERRED the owning-V cost model and CG-CLONE per-entry loop, gated on a future delta that introduces a Clone contract with per-type deep-clone conformers (and a byte-buffer clone for bytes keys).

findings.title: `LEN(r)=LEN(t)` is a relational link fact outside CAT-2's `len=value` vocabulary; hasher instance-state transfer is asserted only in an open question, not the normative row
findings.detail: The facts column produces `LEN(r)=LEN(t)`, a relation between two lengths. CAT-2's LEN family is `len(x)=v` (length equals a value), not len=len; the only precedent for a relational form is seq_as_slice's 'LEN(r)=LEN(s) (link)'. As written the fact is not expressible in the stated fact grammar. Separately, whether the sip_keyed hasher instance state (k0,k1) is cloned — required for the fresh table to hash identically and for lookups to hit — is answered 'yes' only in open question (b), not in the normative row; if omitted the clone is a silently-wrong table (correctness, not safety).
findings.minimal_fix: Mark the fact 'LEN(r)=LEN(t) (link)' matching the seq_as_slice precedent (or add a relational len-link fact to CAT-2). Move the k0/k1-transfer requirement from open-Q (b) into the row's normative text: instance hasher state transfers so equal keys hash equal in the clone.

findings.title: Hardest FAILED attacks — where the drafted rules (or the surrounding kernel) correctly defend
findings.detail: These attacks did NOT break the deltas: (1) len=0 pow2 forgery (x & (0-1) = x, all-ones mask): BLOCKED — the producer `ieq(ipopcount(len),1)` rejects len=0 since ipopcount(0)=0!=1, so the catastrophic all-ones mask is unreachable; len=1 edge is sound (mask 0, x&0=0<1). (2) tbl_clone under a live entry token: BLOCKED — an `entry<'e,K,V>` holds a uniq loan that freezes t (R6/OWN-5), so minting the `&'r t` tbl_clone needs is rejected while the token is live; clone under a live SHARED value-borrow is correctly allowed and safe (read-only). (3) requires-cross-slice forgery (produce a length fact in a helper's prologue, pass a different slice): BLOCKED — FN-8 executes the prologue once per invocation after parameter binding, re-checking the ACTUAL argument's len, so the fact is always about the real callee slice. (4) LOAD-1 returning `own uK` smuggling a lifetime/loan: IMPOSSIBLE — uK is a bare copy primitive with no region or loan; the input's `reads('r)` cannot be laundered through it. (5) A loan-carrying view (seq_as_slice) outliving the fact's invalidation: BLOCKED — the view's shared loan freezes the source seq against any resize for the view's whole lifetime (R6), and the view itself is fixed-length, so a DOM-1 fact on the view is stable while live. The loan layer is doing real defensive work here; the surviving holes (findings 1-8) are precisely where NO loan freezes the place across the fact's window.
findings.minimal_fix: None — recorded so the adoption reviewer can see the attack surface that IS covered and does not relitigate it. The delta authors should keep these five closures explicit when the rules move toward spec.

verdict_per_delta.delta2_tbl_clone: DOES NOT SURVIVE AS-DRAFTED (MAJOR). Core purpose (bulk copy-V clone for C5) is sound and the loan-column NONE + &'r receiver reasoning is correct (clone-under-uniq-token blocked, clone-under-shared allowed). But 'clone' is an undefined predicate (no Clone contract; only copy/affine), the affine-V rejection contradicts the owning-V cost model, owning-V/bytes-K deep clone has no mechanism (shallow fallback = double free), and LEN(r)=LEN(t) is outside CAT-2's fact vocab. Fix: restrict to copy K/V this slice, defer owning-V to a Clone-contract delta. Lightest delta, but not adoptable verbatim.
verdict_per_delta.delta3_load1: DOES NOT SURVIVE AS-DRAFTED (one FATAL, one MAJOR). LOAD-1's base trap predicate `off+K/8 > len(s)` wraps in u64 and fails to trap on attacker-influenced near-max offsets (wild OOB) — a defect in the base op, not the fact channel. The CG 'one unaligned load' is not pinned to align-1 and is UB on strict-alignment targets. LOAD-2 (reinterpret stays scalar) is clean. Fix both predicates to non-wrapping form and pin align-1 lowering; then the op is sound. Returning own uK smuggles nothing.
verdict_per_delta.delta4_dom1: DOES NOT SURVIVE AS-DRAFTED (one FATAL, three MAJOR). This is the dangerous delta and it has the fatal gap the standing rule exists to catch: no cross-call fact havoc, so an opaque &uniq helper can resize a place while the caller's in_bounds fact survives -> elided OP-4 -> OOB (D1 breach). Additionally: (a)'s conclusion has a free variable b and misattributes the bound to pow2 (real premise: len!=0 + mask=current-len(b)-1); (b)'s producer cites a non-existent op, doesn't reject wrapping-add checks, and is unspellable-non-wrapping inside a requires block; and the new fact families are killed only in prose, never wired into the S.1/S.2 kills columns. Intra-procedural DERIVED-INB kills are actually handled by existing rows; the PRODUCER facts and the call boundary are the holes. Must not go to spec until all four are closed and re-attacked.
